page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration includes a 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance with a score of 3144.37, while multi-core performance reaches a strong rating at 100519.25. PassMark CPU and memory scores are also strong, registering at 51157.15 and 2954.39 respectively. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography, which contributes to a strong PassMark encryption test score of 39771.17. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 339.19 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter model. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model allows Vultr to deliver high-density physical core counts and NVMe storage at a lower resource-to-cost ratio compared to dedicated-core instances. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-tenant workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as multi-threaded decompression, lightweight LLM inference, cryptography tasks, and general compute-optimized application hosting.
description: The Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 32 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server includes a 500 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. It lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside strong PassMark CPU and memory scores. The processor supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, enabling high-speed encryption and efficient multi-threaded decompression. With a shared CPU allocation and a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers a cost-effective option for compute-intensive tasks. It is well-suited for multi-threaded processing, cryptography, lightweight machine learning inference, and compute-heavy web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, NVMe storage, and advanced vector instructions for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory.
Bundled 500 GB NVMe SSD local storage.
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and hardware cryptography.
Shared CPU allocation offering high core density.
Best suited for multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and lightweight inference.
